

-- 1 of 15 --



-- 2 of 15 --


www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
How to Find Killer Products
and Make Irresistible Offers –
Every Time!
Why should you read – and HEED – the secrets I reveal in this report?
Simple: Because if you truly want to maximize your results and income as a partner – you will have
to master one of the most often-overlooked and under-appreciated part of every direct response
promotion …
… The Offer.
The way I see it, “The Offer” is much, much more than the price you’re charging for your product or
service. In fact, I count EIGHT major components that great offers share – and we’re going to take a
detailed look at ALL of them in this report.
This is crucial stuff: While most marketers and copywriters spend most of their time developing major
themes, headlines and opening copy, we often sleepwalk through the copy that showcases the offer.
Also, remember that a BIG part of selecting the right offer is knowing how to recognize killer products
– products that practically sell themselves. (I will explain exactly how to locate the best products to
promote later in this report.)
We’re all guilty of undervaluing the search for quality products and offers – and this is a big, BIG boo-
boo: A compelling offer, made with the right product, can easily boost response and average sale
amount by 20 percent, 30 percent or even more!
In contrast, an ill-conceived offer can depress or even kill response – and it can also drive your
fulfillment costs into the stratosphere, leaving your ROI in the dirt.
So grab a cup-o-Joe and get comfortable. Unless I miss my guess, just thinking about the offers you
make on the Internet, in direct mail, in print and in your TV and radio promotions is about to add big
bucks to your bottom line.
Welcome to the days of …
“But Wait – There’s MORE!”
In the old days, merchants and customers hammered out most purchases face to face. The buyer
haggled for the best price and asked the merchant to sweeten the pot by adding something extra.
The advent of mass marketing changed all that, of course. Today, direct response consumers are
accustomed to having your best offer presented to them on a silver platter.
And now as never before, the pressure is ON. With every passing day, our prospects are exposed to
ever-more-amazing offers – especially on TV, in the mail and on the Internet.
Click on your TV and you’re likely to see Ron Popeil giving away what seems like a whole kitchen-full
of free gadgets when you buy his rotisserie oven – or Billy Mays cutting his price in half, doubling the

-- 3 of 15 --

4
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
amount of Orange-Glo you get AND giving you free squee-gees and other goodies.
It’s getting ridiculouser and ridiculouser.
When I first launched Health & Healing back in the early 1990s, we offered a total of four free gifts:
Two with your one-year subscription, one more if you signed up for two years, and one more if you
responded within ten days.
But now, if you were to submit sales copy for a major mailer, chances are that as many as
SEVENTEEN free gifts might come with that health newsletter!
What’s that you say? You don’t sell kitchen gadgets, cleaning products or health newsletters?
Doesn’t matter. The simple truth is that your prospects are being conditioned to expect the moon
– not just by your competitors, but by every direct response marketer out there. Any marketer who
ignores this sea change in consumer attitudes does so at his own peril.
What’s more, if the number of free gifts prospects expect you to offer has more than quadrupled, the
rest of every great offer has also become more intense and complex.
Anatomy of a Power-Packed Offer
Just about every great offer you’ll see is comprised of the eight following components …
(By the way … be sure to USE these as a checklist when you first begin talking to your client about
what they’re selling. They will be blown away by your knowledge and professionalism!)
1. Rationale: Great offers begin with a clear, credible explanation of why you’re about to give away
the farm.
Maybe it’s a “Special Introductory Offer:” You’re so sure that once the prospect experiences the
benefits your product provides, he’ll be a customer for life.
Or, maybe you’re so worried about a current or impending crisis in his health, finances, happiness,
– whatever – that giving him all this good stuff is the only ethical thing an ethical person like you could
possibly do.
Whatever your rationale for the amazing, astonishing, truly spectacular offer you’re about to make,
put it up in lights!
Better yet, use it to position yourself as a concerned advocate and your new customer’s greatest
champion.
2. Discount: Several things to think about here …
FIRST: It’s important to establish your regular price and make it seem like a really good value.
To make your regular price believable, specifics are crucial.
Tell your prospect where your product has been offered or sold for full price or even how many
thousands of folks have paid the full price for the product. (In his Internet promotions, one of my
buddies even includes a link that opens a separate Web page on which the product is offered at full
price!)

-- 4 of 15 --

5
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
Then, demonstrate why even at full price your product is a screaming deal. Show how your product
will save them or make them many times your regular price.
SECOND: Present your discount in a way that dimensionalizes your role as your prospect’s advocate
and champion.
After stating and justifying the regular price, I often say something like, “But it’s so crucial that you get
this help now, I don’t want you to have to pay that much …”
How BIG a discount should you offer? In cases when my client allows me to establish pricing, I
always go for the 50% Introductory Discount.
“You SAVE HALF!” and “HALF-PRICE OFFER” and “HALF-OFF!” have great visceral appeal.
Anything else feels kind of stingy – anything more strains credulity.
THIRD: Take the time to marvel at the piddling amount the prospect will pay. Here again, comparisons
will serve you well.
When possible, quantify the monetary benefits the product will deliver and compare it with the almost
insignificant price.
Break the regular price down to a daily or weekly figure and compare it with something far more
trivial that they spend more on without even thinking about the expense: A gallon of gas, a cup of
Starbucks, etc.
3. Purchase Options: Depending on what you’re selling, determining how many purchase options
you offer the customer can be a thorny decision to make.
The key is to offer a low-end price-point that will get you maximum numbers of new customers, PLUS
one or more higher price-points to increase your average sale and return on investment.
In the newsletter industry for example, we typically offer a one-year and two-year subscription.
(As a rule, the two-year subscription costs somewhere around 1.8 times more than the one-year
subscription. That gives prospects a strong incentive to choose a two-year subscription.)
On some occasions, a three-year option is also offered, usually as an impulse item: “Join me for two
years for just $179 and get a third year for just $19.95!”
When selling nutritional supplements, I’ve found that a maximum of three quantity options is generally
best. Prospects may be invited to order a one-year supply, a six-month supply or a three-month
supply, for example.
The key here is to avoid presenting prospects with too many choices. Your chances of losing the sale
increase with every extra second your prospect spends trying to decide which offer to go for.
4. Payment Terms: For most of my clients’ new customer acquisition promotions, cash with order is
king. But it’s always worth considering the “soft offer” (where the prospect can try the product for free)
– because the response on these kinds of offers can be phenomenal.
For example, I’m told that David DeAngelo – the leading seller of men’s dating information products
on the Web – sells EVERYTHING on a soft offer. The prospect gets to download or receive the
product in the mail, and then he gets a full thirty days to decide if it’s right for him. He will not be billed

-- 5 of 15 --

6
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
until those thirty days are up.
You see, when you’ve got good products that meet your prospect’s needs and/or solve his most
pressing problems … and you work his desire and curiosity up into a frenzy with your sales copy …
… Telling him that he can try the product for thirty days without spending a dime is often impossible to
resist. It goes way beyond even having a strong guarantee because there’s no money down.
Bottom line: When you’re working with your client’s offers – never overlook the possibility of testing a
soft offer. Sure, you may get deadbeats who order and don’t pay – but if your product cost is low, and
you bring in five times more new customers on this kind of offer, who cares if 20% of the buyers don’t
end up paying.
You’ve just made the client a LOT more money!
Also, when crafting promotions for my clients’ customer files, I often consider other types of payment
plans – here are just a few options you may want to consider …
Payment Plan: When your key price-point – the level at which most of your customers will respond
– is relatively rich for prospects’ blood, consider a “ThreePay” or “FourPay” offer.
You collect the credit card number with the order, then bang it for one-third or one-fourth of the total
amount each month for three or four months.
By doing so, you effectively lower the perceived price-point in your prospect’s mind and should
appreciably increase response.
You see this very often right now in the Internet marketing community because the products are often
$1,000 … $2,000 … $3,000 or more.
“’Auto-Bill:” When your products are delivered regularly – weekly or monthly for example, this can
be a great way to go.
You collect the credit card information up front and then bill the card at regular intervals (monthly is
most common) until the customer tells you to stop.
This is often also called a “Continuity Offer” … and the beauty of it is, you can really create powerful
revenue streams for you and your client.
Also remember: One of the best times to make such an offer is at the point of purchase. For example,
if you’re selling a $47 e-book – it’s a good idea to ask your prospect if he wants to join your “Monthly
XX Program” where he’ll receive all this amazing free stuff for just $39 a month. Since he’s just made
a purchase from you, he’s in a very good position to make another one – a purchase that could bring
in substantial revenues over the course of the year.
And for high ticket items, remember that the continuity offers can be more expensive. In the Internet
marketing community, it’s not uncommon for a $2,000 product to be sold – and then invite the
prospect to stay in the program for $200 or $300 per month.
Negative Option: You’ve seen this kind of offer most frequently in book club and CD club promotions.
Each month, the customer is notified of the “Monthly Selection” and an “Alternate Selection.” At that

-- 6 of 15 --

7
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
point, one of three things happens:
√ If he fails to respond, the Monthly Selection is automatically sent and his credit card is
billed.
√ If he indicates he would rather have the Alternate Selection, it is sent and his credit
card is billed.
√ And if he indicates that he does not want any selection that month, nothing is sent and
his credit card is not billed.
The key advantage to all three of these offer types is that each one lowers the perceived price-point.
Instead of signing up for $348-worth of books per year for example, the prospect perceives he is only
signing up for $29 per month.
5. Premiums: These are the free gifts that prospects receive along with the product they are
purchasing.
Typically, in newsletter offers, premiums are timely special reports that provide important information
and advice on topics closely related to the theme of the promotion.
Why are special reports such great premiums? Two reasons:
a. Timely special reports that can promise tremendous profit potential have a far higher
perceived value than a hard-cover book (perceived value: $29.95) or any other free gift
you could possibly offer, and …
b. Printed reports cost only pennies to print and mail, and they can be delivered as
PDFs or e-Books on the Internet for free!
When choosing the topics for the premiums you offer, it’s important to make sure that each one
emphasizes and clarifies your main theme and/or your product’s USP.
It’s also crucial to create a premium structure that makes the highest-priced purchase option far and
away the most appealing to the prospect.
In a campaign for an investment newsletter we just completed, our main theme focused on
tremendous opportunities in natural resource stocks – gold stocks in particular – and our main
premium was a list of the editor’s seven hottest gold stocks.
Two-year subscribers were offered additional reports listing the seven hottest silver and platinum
stocks … the seven greatest energy stocks … and the seven best alternative energy stocks to buy
now.
Prospects were faced with the choice of getting one free gift with a one-year subscription or FOUR
free gifts with a two-year sign-up: A real no-brainer if you ask me – especially since they take no risk
whatsoever with your super-duper …
6. Guarantee: The stronger your guarantee, the better. One-year guarantees tend to be the most
common today. However, I have written Rodale promotions in which you could return a book at
anytime in your lifetime for a full refund!

-- 7 of 15 --

8
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
Cheaping out is the biggest blunder you can make in a guarantee. Offer a 60-day or 90-day
guarantee and you’re going to get a blizzard of refund requests two or three months out.
Delay your customer’s decision date for a year, and you’ll find that refunds fall precipitously.
Whenever possible, tie your guarantee to your product’s performance. Set a specific benchmark:
“XYZ will double your money in gold stocks every three months, or just let me know and I’ll refund
every penny you paid.”
In cases where you have significant production costs – or when there’s a temptation for shady
customers to take advantage of your generosity and get your product for free – you may need to
require that the prospect return the product or “the unused portion” in order to exercise his right to a
refund.
If on the other hand, you’re selling something that costs very little to produce – a newsletter, for
example – you’re better off telling prospects that in the unlikely event they decide to cancel, they can
keep everything they’ve received from you completely without cost or further obligation.
7. Urgency Premiums: Prospects earn these additional free gifts by responding quickly to your offer.
Typically, these “Early-Bird” gifts are given to anyone who responds within 10 days. However, in
Internet promotions, they can be for anyone who orders in the next ten minutes!
And I’ve used another type of urgency premium very successfully over the years …
See, I figure that time is the essence: Every second that passes after I’ve presented my offer is an
opportunity for my prospect to bail out on me.
Normally, in my direct mail promotions, some 60 to 80 percent of my orders arrive by telephone. That
means 20 to 40 percent of my prospects are ordering by snail mail.
And that means they have to take time to fill out the order form, detach it, fold it, put it in the envelope
and drop it into a mailbox.
Now, I’m willing to bet that significant numbers of prospects get part of the way through that process
and then fail to follow through. In short, I sold them, but I’m not getting their orders.
The truth is, I do NOT want them to order by mail. So why not offer an extra premium for using the
phone instead?
Combining a 10-day “Early Bird” bonus PLUS an extra bonus to incentivize folks to order by phone
has been a key component of some of my biggest winners ever.
8. Fulfillment Considerations: We Americans are not known for our patience. Instant gratification is
big for us.
Giving out our credit card info and then cooling our heels for four to six weeks stinks – and I’m willing
to bet that millions of sales are killed every year in this country simply because the prospect assumes
it’ll be a coon’s age before his product and free gifts arrive.
So if you can deliver faster, why not shout it from the rooftops?

-- 8 of 15 --


www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
I have a client who ships his product the very next business day. And I have reams of testimonials
from customers who were amazed that their product arrived just three or four days after they ordered
it.
Think I mention THAT little gem of a fact in my promotions for him? You bet your bippy I do!
Also: If you’re not charging for shipping & handling of your product or premiums, why not put THAT up
in lights? In an industry that routinely charges $6.95 or even more for S&H, your customers will love
you for it!
Get OUT of the Offer Box!
Years ago, as I was happily reviewing the results a new control had produced on its first outing, I had
an idea.
Funny how many little stones can accumulate in your shoes before you notice – but as I looked over
the piece, I realized that one of the premiums had been bothering me all along. Reason: It had almost
no connection at all with the product or the main premium.
I quickly called the client and suggested a test: “Panel A” would be the control as-is – WITH the
oddball premium, and “Panel B” would be the control WITHOUT it.
To my surprise, Panel B won in a landslide. Not only did eliminating that premium lower our fulfillment
cost, it also increased our response rate and average sale!
Turns out, the “HUH?” factor had been turning folks off!
The moral of the story: Allocate cells in every roll-out to test at least one component of your offer. Test
a stronger, longer, more specific guarantee … new titles for your premiums … higher and lower price-
points … new payment options … the works.
I’ll betcha dollars to donuts you’ll be thrilled with the results!
But WAIT – There’s More to Consider …
Thought we were finished? Not by a long shot!
Before you even begin tweaking and tinkering with your client’s offer – you need to be able to
recognize what makes an easily sellable product.
You see, your offer will always be based around a central product.
So it doesn’t really matter how many premiums and discounts you give your prospect … if your
product sucks – he ain’t buying!
Seven Ways to Spot a Likely Killer Product –
BEFORE You Invest Weeks of Your life
And/Or a Not-So-Small Fortune Promoting It
Needless to say, I think long and hard before I decide whether I’m going to promote or launch a new
product. I have some very clear prerequisites in mind — conditions I’m hoping will also help you

-- 9 of 15 --

10
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
select more successful products to promote …
FIRST, you want to make sure the product delivers benefits that significant numbers of prospects are
ALREADY looking for. Use both your research mind and your intuition here.
Do your due diligence. Does all the investigating, interviewing and hunting around tell you that people
are looking for this kind of product? (or at the very least – are they actively searching for the benefits
your potential product delivers?)
And when your research still leaves some doubt, put yourself in the prospect’s shoes and think, “If I
were the prospect, why would I want this? Am I really looking for this benefit? Is it on the forefront of
my mind?”
Only after you can answer those questions positively should you proceed any further.
SECOND, you want a product that really works. If you’re selling nutritional supplements, for example,
that means a product with an active ingredient that has been proven to help people in studies
conducted by scores of major medical institutions and that offers you tons of testimonials and case
studies to use in your promotion.
And it’s the same with any other type of product – look closely for strong proof elements like case
histories … studies … powerful third party endorsements … compelling factual data … and so on.
Often times it’s more important to have the most PROVEN product on the market than it is to have the
best one.
Because in these skeptical times, proof will make you stand out more than just about anything else.
THIRD, it’s always best to find something new; something that hasn’t been widely promoted before.
That gives you the opportunity to instantly establish market dominance in your niche before your
successful promotions cause an army of competitors to flood into the marketplace.
And remember – your product can always be a new spin on achieving an old benefit. For example,
think about weight loss. Millions of people are seeking this benefit and have so for hundreds of years
… and still new weight loss products are coming out every day.
So what makes a good one versus a bad one? Well it’s pretty simple – the good ones are usually an
entirely new spin on the process of losing weight. It may be a new miracle nutrient that can’t be found
elsewhere … a new diet by a hotshot doctor … a breakthrough exercise system … the list goes on.
Your benefit doesn’t have to be new – but the way that your product allows the prospect to experience
and realize this benefit most certainly should be!
FOURTH, look for a product that is proprietary; hopefully a patented formulation, method or system
that competitors could not duplicate.
A big part of this comes down to how you systematize and present the product. For example, let’s say
you’re selling a marketing product for dentists, a product that allows dentists to double their clientele
by using a referral system.
You might name it something like … The 2-Step “1+1” Referral System for Dentists … and then go on
to explain how your method is totally unique – and exactly why it causes the prospect’s business to
be flooded with referral customers.

-- 10 of 15 --

11
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
Which brings up a good point – if the product you find seems solid but perhaps doesn’t appear to be
unique enough … you might just need to re-name and re-position it in a way that creates a high level
of perceived uniqueness.
FIFTH, in order to attract the largest number of prospects possible, you want a product that does
more than just one thing. With supplements, you want something that addresses not just one, but
several of your prospect’s most common health problems.
In a marketing product – it should not only make him money … it should save time … make his life
easier … and so on.
This is where it pays in spades to know your prospects really well – and why you MUST do a great
job of breaking down all the benefits a particular product offers. That way, you can match up your
product’s benefits to the prospect’s various desires.
This will give you a MUCH HIGHER probably of picking a winner in advance.
SIXTH, in order to create intense interest in your product, you want it to address problems that scare
him the most and/or that have the greatest negative impact on our enjoyment of life.
For example – if your product is about earning more income as a real estate agent – make sure it
amplifies and solves the fear he’s experiencing about the economy, or that his competition will eat him
alive – or anything else he’s fearing.
In the health market, the greatest fears are often dying from a heart complication or disease –
combined with the fear about what will happen to family and loved ones.
Of course, it’s going to be different for every product – and the key thing to make sure of is that your
product address issues, challenges and problems that your prospect has VERY strong feelings about.
And SEVENTH, you want a product that has something to offer folks who are already suffering with
the main problem your product addresses and that also offers others hope of preventing the kinds of
future problems we all have nightmares about …
… For example, if your product is going to make online business owners more money – you might
want to also emphasize that it’s something that will get and KEEP him out of debt for the rest of his
life.
Why? Because that’s a problem that many such business owners have a deep, underlying fear about.
Or let’s say that he’s looking to grow his offline storefront business and generate lots of foot traffic
… If that’s the case, be sure you also remind him about how this is the magic missing ingredient for
preventing his business from failing … a plight that sentences 95% of all small businesses to doom.
There’s more, of course. From the outset, you should make sure your product also …
√ Is reasonably priced relative to the benefits it offers …
√ Offers your client a significant-enough mark-up to enable him to rapidly expand the
marketing budget as the sales begin to roll in, and …
√ Provides maximum potential for on-going sales to existing customers and also create

-- 11 of 15 --

12
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
the opportunity to sell other complementary products to them.
So, with all that said …
How About YOU?
Do you have a system for identifying the products that are most likely to create a grand-slam for you?
No?
Well … now you do! If you can answer yes to the various elements I’ve just described –
congratulations! You’ve found a great product to promote.
Maybe it’s a client’s product; maybe it’s your own.
It could be an information product – a book or e-book, newsletter or website membership, an audio or
video, a live teleseminar or webinar, a course, conference or a live coaching service – that really does
help folks get richer, healthier, thinner, sexier or just plain happier.
Or, maybe it’s a widget – a gadget that slices and dices … or fricassees your chicken in record time
… or effortlessly cleans your carpet or de-wrinkles your clothes … or magically transforms a drooping
derriere into buns of steel or turns a beer-keg belly into a rock-hard six-pack.
It could even be a service of some kind – something that does things for customers that they can’t do
or would rather not do for themselves.
And because your discovery possesses the seven qualities grand-slam homerun products share, you
suspect you just might have found the ultimate direct response product:
1. It delivers benefits your prospects already want: Or better yet, It delivers benefits
that tens of millions of consumers already know they desperately need in their lives.
What’s more, your prospects crave those benefits on an emotional level. Your product
fulfills their most passionate desires … eliminates their most irksome frustrations … and/
or allays their most nightmarish fears.
2. It conveys and delivers these rational and emotional benefits in superior ways:
Your product delivers these benefits faster, easier, with less guesswork, more surely
and/or more completely than any competing product can.
3. It’s totally unique and/or proprietary: The product is truly one of a kind – and
in some important way, there’s nothing else out there quite like it. In a perfect world
(though this is not always realistic), there is no competition. It creates a whole new niche
by filling a need that no other product yet has.
4. You’ve got proof elements out the wazzoo: Not only do top experts in the
niche rave, so do customers – and you’ve got reams of third-party test results,
expert endorsements and articles … customer surveys … testimonials … and other
ammunition to prove it.
5. It’s a screamin’ deal: The regular price is insignificant compared to the benefits your
product delivers. And of course, if your prospect could get similar benefits anywhere
else, he’d have to pay much, much more.

-- 12 of 15 --

13
www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
6. The offer makes buying this product, from you today, a no-brainer: You’re
offering an introductory discount, plus free gifts that further trivialize the price and
magnify the value prospects get just for ordering.
Plus, you’re offering an extra bonus or discount just for ordering now.
And your iron-clad guarantee or “try-it-before-you-buy-it” offer assures prospects that
they risk absolutely, positively nothing when they give your product a fair trial.
7. Downstream sales are a slam-dunk: The product is consumed or must be updated
regularly. Or, it’s the first in a series of products in the same niche your new customers
will want to buy from you.
In fact, this product is so good, you’d feel guilty if you failed to nag your sweet, sainted
old grammy until she bought it. Or better yet, you’d joyfully buy it and give it to her
yourself.
Not only that, but you and your client have put together an offer that is to die for – one so juicy that no
sane prospect would refuse.
Congratulations, my friend: You’ve come to a magical place that can make your reputation, your
career and your fortune.
… So where do you start?
Well, you’d sit right down and write the ultimate promotion for your ultimate product and offer – right?
Well, not exactly. In fact, not by a long shot.
That would be, like, “Fire! Ready? Aim.”
First, you need to find a marketing model that’s the best fit for your product.
“So what the heck is a marketing model?” you ask.
That’s exactly what we’re going to cover in another one of your free reports!
So go through the ”Overnight Marketing Genius” Checklist on the next page and then we’ll roll up our
sleeves and jump into the world of marketing models …

-- 13 of 15 --

14
The Makepeace Business System
How to Find Killer Products and Make Irresistible Offers – Every Time!
Your “Overnight Marketing Genius” Checklist
The next time you’re considering working with a client (or perhaps you’ve just started) – you now
know two of the most important factors in your success: Your product and offer.
Use the following questions as a checklist to ensure you cover all the bases – and radically improve
the chances that you’ll select a winner before you pour lots of time and energy into any kind of
promotion.
Here they are …
When Considering the Product:
√ Does it work? Are there plenty of third-party endorsements, case studies and testimonials to bring
credibility to your promotion?
√ Is it new? Does it have news value?
√ Is it impossible or at least difficult for competitors to duplicate?
√ Does it provide more than one valuable benefit?
√ Is the result the product delivers desperately desired by prospects?
√ Does it enfranchise people who are looking to both prevent and to solve a problem?
√ Is the price reasonable relative to the benefits the product delivers?
√ Is the mark-up sufficient to support a rapidly growing marketing campaign?
√ Does the product come with a raft of related products that will allow you to immediately begin
generating additional revenue from your new customers?
And once you can successfully answer yes to many (ideally ALL) of these questions – then focus on
clarifying the details of your offer …
When Thinking About the Offer:
1. Rationale: Great offers begin with a clear, credible explanation of why you’re about to give away
the farm. So why are you making such an amazing offer?
2. Discount: Once, you’ve established your regular price and made it seem like a really good value
… tell him that he’ll be getting a huge discount – chicken feed relative to the amazing benefits you’re
offering …
3. Purchase Options: Depending on what you’re selling, you’re going to have to get crystal clear on if
you’re going to make an additional sale … allow him to get a discount for buying multiple units … and
so forth. (Remember to avoid presenting prospects with too many choices.)
4. Payment Terms: Are you going to be making a hard offer … a soft offer … using a payment plan
… or anything else that affects how he pays? Figure out what’s going to be most appealing to your
customers and then take action!

-- 14 of 15 --



-- 15 of 15 --
